title: Daily Briefing — May 08
headline: Tech’s running while the rest limps; flows show the split, so don’t chase—abundance mindset.
body: Right, so let’s look at what the flows are telling us: SPY holds strong at $731.58, well above the 20 MA ($710.16), with QQQ showing similar strength. VIX is drifting below its 20 MA—volatility contracting, but sentiment is stuck in greed (66.8). Markets are still bullish, but breadth is thinning; tech carries the torch while most cyclicals lag.

Sector rotation is screaming that XLK (Tech) remains the lone leader (RS=113.3, Momentum=105.1), while everything else—XLE, XLI, XLB, XLF, XLV, XLP—is lagging hard and showing high risk. Communication (XLC) and Consumer Discretionary (XLY) are improving relative strength, but the rest are still rolling over. XLF at $51.55 sits just above the 20 MA ($51.87)—financials are the canary, and they’re barely hanging on.

Contrarian warning: Fear & Greed is high but not extreme (66.8); last month was extreme fear (34.4), so retail has poured back in quickly. Watch out for overexposure—most names are extended well above the 20 MA (e.g., AAPL $287.44 vs. SMA20 $271.57), and laggards are breaking down. HYG, LQD, and TLT all show bearish signals. If bonds freak out, everything breaks—reduce equity risk if these start closing below their weekly 20 MA.
callout_body:
SPY: $731.58 (SMA20 $710.16) — strong tech leadership
QQQ: $694.94 (SMA20 $655.79) — momentum intact
XLF: $51.55 (SMA20 $51.87) — canary at the wire
HYG: $79.86 (SMA20 $79.97) — risk zone
AAPL: $287.44 (approaching SMA20 $271.57)
Fear & Greed: 66.8 — caution, but not extreme
Watch for daily closes below 20 MA for exit triggers
action_items: Rotate into XLK (Tech) only on pullbacks to weekly 20 MA—don’t chase extended moves
Monitor XLF and HYG for breakdowns—if either closes below the 20 MA, reduce equity exposure across portfolio
closing_quote: If it’s in the press, it’s in the price. Patience, react, don’t predict.
